ps140: Rome puts you forward as a blossoming rose of sweet fragrance,* perfuming the thoughts of the faithful with the scent of the virtues* and ever dispelling the stench of the passions by grace,* O much-suffering Agrippina, adornment of the martyrs,* confirmation of the Church, boast of virgins* and abyss of miracles.

You, who suffered in Rome,* Christ our God has given to Sicily as riches beyond count;* and having arrived there, O glorious martyr,* you drive away the evil multitude of demons by your intercession.* Therefore, we bless you and celebrate your suffering today,* O much-suffering Agrippina.

Bassa and Paula bore you on their shoulders* at the command of Him who bears all things,* moving you from place to place* and making a long journey over the deep.* And working awesome miracles through divine grace, O martyr Agrippina,* you came to rest in the place which God had appointed,* becoming a place of rest for the heavy-laden, O most lauded one.
theotokion: O Mistress, take pity on me* who am bowed down beneath the assaults of the demons* and cast into the pit of destruction,* and establish me upon the rock of the virtues;* and dispelling the attacks of the enemy,* grant that I may keep the commandments of your Son and our God,* that I may receive remission on the day of judgment.
stavrotheotokion: The all-pure one, beholding Christ crucified, who loves mankind,* His side pierced by a spear, cried out, weeping:* What is this, O my Son?* How have the ungrateful people rewarded You for the good You have done them?* How is it that You are in haste to leave me childless, O most Beloved?* I marvel at Your voluntary crucifixion, O Compassionate One.
